Two sets of seven spherical simulated lesions with diameters of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6.3, 7.9, and 9.5 mm have <jats:italic>T<\/jats:italic><jats:sub>1<\/jats:sub> and <jats:italic>T<\/jats:italic><jats:sub>2<\/jats:sub> values somewhat higher than the corresponding values in the surrounding simulated normal tissue. Relaxometer determinations of <jats:italic>T<\/jats:italic><jats:sub>1<\/jats:sub> and <jats:italic>T<\/jats:italic><jats:sub>2<\/jats:sub> for the simulated normal tissue yielded 955 and 106 ms, respectively, at 22 \u00b0C and 1 T. The corresponding values in set \u266f1 of the simulated lesions were 1017 and 175 ms and in set \u266f2 were 1002 and 127 ms. These <jats:italic>T<\/jats:italic><jats:sub>1<\/jats:sub> and <jats:italic>T<\/jats:italic><jats:sub>2<\/jats:sub> values are similar to those for brain and brain lesions but are too high to represent soft tissue such as liver and muscle. The centers of the 14 lesions are coplanar, and eight alignment devices surround them facilitating superposition of the scan plane and the plane containing the centers of the lesions. Illustrative images made with a 1.5\u2010T system are shown. All simulated lesions are detectable in <jats:italic>T<\/jats:italic><jats:sub>2<\/jats:sub>\u2010weighted head coil images. Corresponding <jats:italic>T<\/jats:italic><jats:sub>1<\/jats:sub>\u2010weighted images are also shown, the 2\u2010 and 3\u2010mm lesions of set \u266f2 and the 2\u2010mm lesion of set \u266f1 not being detectable. The phantoms could be useful for performance or acceptance testing and perhaps for quality assurance testing.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.1118\/1.596659","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2003,3,28]],"date-time":"2003-03-28T15:37:52Z","timestamp":1048865872000},"page":"549-554","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":10,"title":["Low\u2010contrast
